_note: BRIDGES: four flagged links that stitch isolated king-list islands into the main tree (and consolidate China internally). These are NOT bloodline. Each connecting family carries kind:bridge (renders faint/dashed) and its bridge node's label states the mechanism: SUCCESSION, CONQUEST, or FOUNDING. To avoid double-parenting, every node that GAINS a parent here was verified to be a ROOT (no existing parent) before this file was written: BAB_SUMUABUM, IN_BIMBISARA, CN_ZHAOXIANG, CN_GAOZU, CAR_DIDO. The tail nodes on the parent side (SUM_URNINURTA, IN_KURU, CN_PING, CN_HUHAI, PHO_ITHOBAAL1) keep their own existing families; they only gain an extra outward marriage to a placeholder spouse. (1) SUMER->BABYLON: the Isin usurper Ur-Ninurta (c.1923) -> the Amorite-kingdoms bridge -> Sumu-abum, founder of Babylon's First Dynasty (c.1894, ROOT) — Babylon rose amid the Isin-Larsa Sumerian south, which Hammurabi absorbed by conquering Rim-Sin of Larsa in 1763 BC; SUCCESSION, not descent. (2) PURANIC INDIA->THE MAURYAS: Kuru (c.2400, Puranic tail) -> the Puranic-dynasties bridge -> Bimbisara of Magadha (c.558, ROOT, the Haryanka->Shishunaga->Nanda->Maurya entry) — LEGENDARY/schematic succession the Puranas draw from the post-Mahabharata kings down to the Mauryas. (3) CHINA (internal only, NOT connected to the West): Mandate-of-Heaven succession by conquest — King Ping of Zhou (c.770, Zhou tail) -> Spring-&-Autumn/Warring-States bridge -> King Zhaoxiang of Qin (c.306, ROOT of the Qin line that produced Qin Shi Huang), and Huhai/Qin Er Shi (c.230, Qin tail) -> Chu-Han-Contention bridge -> Emperor Gaozu of Han (Liu Bang, ROOT). NOTE: Gaozu's fixed label reads 256-195 BC, so his parsed birth-year predates Huhai's; this is inherent to a dynastic-succession bridge (Qin fell 207 BC, Han founded 202 BC), NOT descent, and the layout's lane logic still forces him below the bridge. (4) CARTHAGE->PHOENICIA/TYRE: the documented Tyrian king-list (Menander of Ephesus via Josephus) is extended as real father-son descent — Ithobaal I -> Baal-Eser II -> Mattan I — then Mattan I's children Pygmalion (stays in Tyre) & Elissa/Dido are attached via a FOUNDING bridge, since Dido (ROOT) fled her brother Pygmalion of Tyre to found Carthage in 814 BC. Web-verified 2026-07-16.

children.label:
the Amorite kingdoms
c.1900 BC · Babylon rose amid the Isin-Larsa Sumerian south, & Hammurabi absorbed the south by conquering Rim-Sin of Larsa in 1763 BC · SUCCESSION / CONQUEST, not descent

the Puranic dynasties
c.1200 BC · the Puranas trace the post-Mahabharata kings (Barhadrathas → Pradyotas → Shishunagas → Nandas) down to the Mauryas · LEGENDARY / schematic SUCCESSION, not descent

the Spring & Autumn & Warring States
c.350 BC · as Zhou authority waned, the state of Qin rose & conquered its rivals to unify China · the MANDATE OF HEAVEN passing by CONQUEST, not bloodline

the Chu-Han Contention
c.206 BC · after Qin's collapse in 207 BC, Liu Bang defeated Xiang Yu & founded the Han in 202 BC · the MANDATE OF HEAVEN passing by CONQUEST, not bloodline

👑 Baal-Eser II (Baal-Mazzer II)
c.856 BC · King of Tyre, son of Ithobaal I; paid tribute to Shalmaneser III in 841 BC · from the Tyrian king-list of Menander of Ephesus (via Josephus) · historical

👑 Mattan I
c.840 BC · King of Tyre, son of Baal-Eser II; father of Pygmalion & of Elissa (Dido) · historical

👑 Pygmalion of Tyre
c.831 BC · King of Tyre, son of Mattan I, whose murder of Elissa's husband Acerbas drove her to flee & found Carthage · historical
